title: Level 334
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:
Level 334: "Neverending Vending"
}} {{Background|file=Vending3.jpg}}
{{Class 2|color-h=0.1|color-l=2%}} ==> Description== {{Blurimage|file=Vending2.webp|caption=Anxious.}} '''Level 334''' consists of a boundless office building with a seemingly haphazard layout. The rooms are mostly devoid of furniture, with the only present non-discarded objects being the levels' unique vending machines. They are randomly scattered around rooms and hallways, some can even be found lying broken on the floor. The machines can dispense any types of snacks and drinks which are/were found within [[the Frontrooms]], even the ones from older periods and the ones which aren't produced anymore. Turning the vending machines on is relatively easy. It can be done by inserting any object which is shaped like a coin in the small coin dispenser. Strangely enough, the machines lack any coin identification system, with any coin or coin-shaped object being suitable for buying. It is speculated that the vending machines are impossible to break and that they are made out of a supernatural material that reflects any damage done to the machine. Many people have tried to hit them using strong items, although they reported that they felt immense pain and bleeding like they were the ones hit with a hammer. Most of the machines have an infinite supply of products inside them, possessing the ability to refill themselves every time they are out of the sight of a person. Electricity and power do not matter to the vending machines. They can work even if they are plugged out. The contents of the snacks and drinks vary from machine to machine, thus it isn't the best choice to drink/eat them. Before ordering anything one should check the expiry date to prevent diseases or sickness. {{Blurimage|file=1280.jpg|caption=Twisting office corridor with vendors.}} The level is dimly lit, with there being almost no light present. The vending machines produce a slight glow, which is the most light that can be found at this level. Most entities are attracted to the light, thus it would be better to not approach it. '''Level 334''' has an abundance of fire alarms and outlets, but they don't work if one attempts to activate them, making them seemingly useless. If a wanderer goes too far they will be greeted doors with vibrant exit signs. They usually lead the wanderer back to where they started, but a few people have reported that they were sent to random [[Level List|levels]]. The rooms also are filled with windows that show dark silhouettes of cityscapes that are seemingly devoid of any life. '''Level 334''' doesn't change temperature, instead, it always stays at a slightly cold -20 degrees Fahrenheit. Wallpaper is commonly present on the walls, although it is usually found in bad condition. {{Blurimage|file=Vendingmachine.webp|caption=Menacing Glow}} While wandering, one may hear very quiet songs. They sound like normal songs from the Frontrooms but with non-distinguishable lyrics. No one knows the source of the sound, as it always sounds far away no matter how deep one gets in the level. Some have speculated that it is an entity, or a radio, but there is no concrete evidence proving any of these claims. Posters are also commonly found while traversing '''Level 334.''' They reference the many brands of sodas and snacks that the level vending machines may dispense. Some of them even advertise objects and locations from the Backrooms itself, like [[The Game]], [[Object 1|Victoria's Almond Water]], [[Level 16|J. Barry Mental Institute]], [[The Delta Mall]], and among many others. These posters are unique due to them being able to regenerate if a person or entity attempts to peel them off or damage them. '''Level 334''' has a slow internet speed which isn't very reliable as it occasionally runs out. Weirdly enough, many websites will be blocked if you use the internet within '''Level 334'''. These are mostly ones relating to the '''Backrooms''', or '''Level 334''' itself. '''Level 334''' doesn't only consist of the vending machine-filled hallways and small rooms, it also contains:
  • Cafeterias
  • Restrooms
{{Blurimage|file=E8nvG1ZXIAgz5VT.jpg|caption=Shadowy lunchroom.}} Cafeterias are rare rooms that are usually the bases of colonies, outposts, or normal wanderers. They are normally encountered behind double doors with vibrant signs above them saying "CAFETERIA" or "CANTEEN". When one enters the cafeteria they will be greeted by many empty seats and tables which are almost completely devoid of food, with the only food present being most likely expired. The kitchens are usually inaccessible because of them being locked. They can be entered if one uses any strong item to break the doors. Stalls are also usually impossible to access, with people speculating that they are empty.
{{Blurimage|file=Wjz6gjrjrmk61.webp|caption=Vacant bathrooms.}} Restrooms/Bathrooms at '''Level 334''' are planned strangely; some bathrooms aren't even individual rooms but whole areas that are just comprised of sinks and toilets. The sinks don't dispense water, instead, they dispense the same soda brands as the vending machines. Their layout is extremely strange, and some of them are even bigger than cafeterias. The reason for such planning remains unknown. This is the only known area of the level that doesn't have any vending machines (if you don't count the sinks, that is).
{{Blurimage|file=Vending3.webp|caption=I feel like I have company.}} ==> Entities== '''Level 334''' has a few entities, although it is easy to escape them and hide due to the level having barely any lights at all. The most common entities that can be encountered within '''Level 334''' are [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 2|Windows]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/Level_-4#Entities Snares] and [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]]. There have been reported sightings of entities such as [[Phenomenon 35|Wretches]], [[Needlelimbs]], and [[Slashers]]. These are only speculated with no proof, so they most likely are just hoaxes. ==> Colonies and Outposts== ==='''Outpost Petra'''=== The '''Outpost Petra''' is the most known colony/outpost in '''Level 334.''' It is named after the person who discovered '''Level 334,''' Felipe Petra. They are known for helping wanderers. They are open to trading if you give them what they want. They are composed of '''27''' estimated members. There have been many other outposts, although they usually have less than ten members and aren't worthy of mentioning. ==> Entrances and Exits== ==='''Entrances'''=== * The only known way to enter Level 334 is by noclipping through random vending machines. This does not always work. ==='''Exits'''=== * Certain lunchrooms lead to [[Level 610]]. * No-clipping through any window of crimson shade will lead to [[Level 93]]. * Entering a kitchen holding a Delta Mall poster may teleport one to [[The Delta Mall]]. ==> Misc== ==='''Discovery'''=== Felipe Petra discovered the level on March 10, 1992. {{Redirect|Next=335|Prev=333}}
